title: The Intelligent Investor
subtitle: The Definitive Book on Value Investing
author: Benjamin Graham
authorBio: Benjamin Graham (1894–1976) was a British-American financial analyst, investor, and professor. Widely known as the father of value investing, he taught at Columbia Business School and co-authored Security Analysis with David Dodd. His most famous student was Warren Buffett. Graham founded the Graham-Newman partnership and pioneered the discipline of security analysis based on fundamental metrics, intrinsic value, and the margin of safety.
notableQuotes: The intelligent investor is a realist who sells to optimists and buys from pessimists.
You are neither right nor wrong because the crowd disagrees with you. You are right because your data and your reasoning are right.
The individual investor should act consistently as an investor and not as a speculator.
The intelligent investor should recognize that market fluctuations are his friend, not his enemy.
The margin of safety is always dependent on the price paid.
The stock market is a voting machine in the short term and a weighing machine in the long term.
warrenBuffettQuote: By far the best book about investing ever written.
